name: YaRN
oneliner: Improved RoPE extension: divides frequency bands into three zones (untouched / ramped / fully interpolated) plus attention temperature scaling. Achieves 64-128$\times$ extension with ~400 fine-tuning steps.
explanation: Yet Another RoPE Extension improves on NTK-aware scaling by treating different frequency bands in the position encoding separately rather than applying a single smooth adjustment. High-frequency bands encoding local positions are left untouched, low-frequency bands encoding global positions are fully interpolated, and a transition zone gets a smooth ramp between the two. YaRN also applies temperature scaling to attention logits to counter entropy shifts. It achieved 32x context extension on Llama 2 with only about 400 training steps.
fundamentals: Define $r_i$ = $\lambda_i$/L (wavelength/context ratio). Thresholds $\alpha$=1, $\beta$=32. If $r_i$<$\alpha$: no scaling. If $r_i$>$\beta$: full interpolation (÷s). Between: ramp $\gamma_i$ = ($r_i$-$\alpha$)/($\beta$-$\alpha$), scale = 1/(1+$\gamma_i$·(s-1)). Temperature: attention *= $\sqrt{1/t}$ where t $\approx$ 0.1·ln(s)+1. Three-part approach preserves local precision while extending global range — key improvement over uniform NTK. Extension: LLaMA 2 7B/13B to 64K-128K, passkey retrieval passing at 128K.
sources: Peng et al., 'YaRN: Efficient Context Window Extension of Large Language Models,' 2023, arXiv:2309.00071

foundational_papers.title: YaRN: Efficient Context Window Extension of Large Language Models
